ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ tšé̱ heni egbĩ le̱ šikpoṅ, ni nui le̱ anābuamo̱ le̱ etšé̱le̱ ṅšo̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehĩ.
translated: I am Nyonmo̱, I came from the sky, I have come to bring blessings and good things. I am Nyonmo̱, I have arrived.

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: ekē̱: Alomo̱ Kanaan, nyṍdši anyṍṅ eetšṍ ehā enyemimei le̱!
translated: Oh Canaanite merchants, your eyes have been opened by your money!

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Ni Laban ṅõ eweyō Zilpa ehã ebi Lea, ni efe eweyõ.
translated: Laban gave his daughter Leah to Jacob, and also gave her his servant Zilpah.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni dše tše̱re le̱, na, Lea ni. Ni ekē̱ Laban ake̱: Meni ofemi ne̱? Ani dšee Rahel hewo̱ ni misúmo̱o le̱? Mẽhewo̱ mo̱ ni ošišiumi ne̱?
translated: I asked my mother, "Why are you doing this to me? Wasn't I serving you for Rachel? So why have you deceived me?"

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Ni edšō̱le̱ ni ekē̱: Aadšō̱ Abram ahã ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ ni dši ṅwei ke̱ šikpoṅ tše̱ le̱;
translated: And God said to Abram: Leave your country, your relatives, and your father's house, and go to the land that I will show you;
